title: Brownfield Access Review Architecture from Reviewed Requirements
prompt: Read `./access-review-modernization-requirements.md`. Work only from that file. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, state them briefly and continue. Produce a system-design artifact for release 1 that preserves brownfield coexistence with the legacy module, keeps unresolved questions visible, and stays in the architecture layer rather than jumping into implementation, cutover, schema, or service decomposition details.

assertions.name: stays-in-architecture-phase
assertions.description: Keeps the output in architecture and does not collapse into implementation, cutover sequencing, schema design, or service decomposition

assertions.name: preserves-brownfield-coexistence
assertions.description: Preserves the release-1 coexistence boundary with the legacy module instead of assuming a full rewrite or same-day cutover

assertions.name: preserves-upstream-open-questions
assertions.description: Keeps unresolved tenant, historical-data, and synchronization questions visible rather than silently deciding them

assertions.name: maps-drivers-to-structure
assertions.description: Names architectural drivers before choosing structure and ties decisions back to those drivers

assertions.name: prepares-downstream-handoff
assertions.description: Produces an artifact shaped for downstream `api-design`, `data-modeling`, or `task-breakdown` work
